rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AIKO ONODERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/14/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The accounting period is for the time the co-administrators acted; Hiroki Onodera, the other co-administrator did not sign or verify either the petition or the "Response to Tentative Ruling" (which should just have been a SUPPLEMENT to the petition, pursuant to CRC 7.3.) No financial statement has been filed yet for the Sentinel Funds (Touchstone Investments). How did the accounting balance, if 10 months of water bills were missing for both properties? For what services were accounting fees paid on 11/27/12? Why was Co-administrator Elizabeth Onodera reimbursed for payroll on 1/23/13? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 2/2/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF BERNARD M FOSTON SR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/7/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Proof of payment of the assessment fee must be filed before an accounting can be approved. An appearance may be required on 2/7/23 for the Court to determine what to do about disbursements made without a court order for "living fee", allowance, and mileage and "fees" to the now deceased conservator and her husband. Also, many reimbursements were made without any indication as to the original payee, numerous gifts to family members were made, and the estate paid for public storage and an automobile, although no tangible personal property was inventoried. No bank statements have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (How was the successor conservator able to (re)create the accounting activities without access to them?) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 1/27/23 to be considered. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERALD R. BARRICK
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (Undeveloped Land In Novato, Marin County)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issue must be cured prior to the hearing. 1)Reappraisal Inventory & Appraisal has not been filed. PC 10309. Petition alleges that reappraisal value within one year before the hearing is $1,200,000; however, the most recent I&A was filed on 8/16/2019. PC 10309. 2) Deposit $36,000 is 3% of the amount bid. Need 10% deposit. 3) Attachment 4f to set forth the terms of sale. A photocopy of the entire purchase agreement to the petition and the order appears insufficient as the court will not review the entire agreement. Petition #4.f and Proposed Order need to be updated. Order cannot be signed as presented. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIGUEL Q URIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Michelle D Urias Verified Petition For Approval Of The Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/22/23. (No earlier availability.) The only correct form of notice for a decedent's estate petition is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120. The proof of service is on the reverse side of that form. If a pleading form of proof of service is completed, a copy of the Notice of Hearing must be attached to that proof of service. If the decedent's mother, Maria Elena Hidalgo died on3/7/21, she could not have participated in the settlement. Her successor-in-interest (personal representative or trustee or intestate heir(s)) was/were not represented. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 2/10/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESUSITA A. DOMATOL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Terminate Trust, To Determine Ownership Of Property, And For Transfer Of The Property To The Probate Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/22/23. (No earlier availability.) This appears to be essentially the same petition that was denied on 7/14/22 but for a reference to authority pertaining to trusts. An appearance will be required on 2/22/23. The reviewing examiner has been unable to find anything in the records indicating whether or not the decedent's predeceased brother was survived by issue. He was listed in item 8 of the Petition for Probate with just his date of death, and no supplement to that petition appears to have been filed. This should now be cleared up by a supplement to this petition. If Pedro Ambalong was survived by issue, they were entitled to notice at the outset of these proceedings. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 2/10/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROLYN E ROBINSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/30/2023 (no earlier date available): 1) No courtesy copies have been received. 2) The notice mailed and published must refer to the "amended" petition where applicable. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed conventionally and confidentially with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). LRSF 14.18(E). 4) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/30/23 to be heard with competing petition. (Is the petitioner the corporation or the individual? Item 3g(2) of the petition is unclear, as the petitioner appears to be the corporation, not the spouse. The corporation/petitioner is missing from item 8 of the petition. This Court requires an ex parte petition to be filed to dispense with notice to parties whose whereabouts are unknown. Has the proposed administrator consented to serve?)
